chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّجُلِ يَطُوفُ فَتَعْرِضُ لَهُ الْحَاجَةُ أَوِ الْعِلَّةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 129 – The man performs Tawaaf, so a need presents itself to him, or an illness

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/32)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/32)
text: رَجُلٍ طَافَ شَوْطاً أَوْ شَوْطَيْنِ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ مَعَ رَجُلٍ فِي حَاجَةٍ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ طَوَافَ نَافِلَةٍ بَنَى عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ طَوَافَ فَرِيضَةٍ لَمْ يَبْنِ عَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel, from Aban Bin Taghlub,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding a man who performs Tawaaf one circuit, or two circuits, then goes out with a man regarding a need. So heasws said: ‘If it was such that it’s was an optional Tawaaf, he would build upon it (and complete the seven circuits), but if it was such that it was an Obligatory Tawaaf, he would not build upon it (i.e., repeat it)’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil from Aban ibn Taghlib who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who performs one or two Tawaf (walking around the House), then goes out with someone for a certain work. He (the Imam) said, Tf it is an optional Tawaf, he can resume where he has left; but if it is an obligatory Tawaf he cannot continue from where he has left.”’
